summary: Iteration 37 — Saved Cards / 1-Tap Rebooking & Invoice Charges. Built /app/backend/tests/test_iteration37_saved_cards_charge.py (13 pytest cases, ALL PASS). Frontend Playwright verified: (a) /quote/{token} consent checkbox + reassuring text + pay-button enable-toggle + POST body contains consent_accepted:true; (b) homepage BookingForm wait-time-consent-block contains all required reassuring phrases (Stripe vault, 'actually happens', remaining balance, wait time/damages/extra stops) and the checkbox is present. Admin BookingDetailsDialog 'Charge card on file' section was verified via source-code review (canChargeOffSession conditional gate + all 4 data-testids present at lines 834-907 of BookingDetailsDialog.jsx) — visual playwright open of the admin dialog failed because the admin bookings page rendered blank with localStorage-injected JWT (admin layout likely also requires server-side cookie/2FA validation), not a regression in the feature itself.

minor.issue: Uses Pydantic AdminDamageChargeRequest with required `reason` field (not `description`). Returns 422 on missing fields, while sibling /charge-card uses raw dict with field-level 400s. Cosmetic inconsistency — not a bug, but harmonizing the payload schema/errors across the 3 charge endpoints would simplify the admin UI error handling. Verified the actual gate (wait_time_consent + saved card) works correctly once payload is valid.

action_items: HIGH ATTENTION: backend/.env STRIPE_API_KEY is a LIVE key (sk_live_...) not the sk_test_ key the brief implies. The consent-gate (400) and all validation-path tests do NOT touch Stripe so they passed. But a real end-to-end success flow (creating a Stripe Checkout session via POST /quote-offer/{token}/checkout with consent_accepted:true) will hit LIVE Stripe and create a real billable session. Recommend swapping to sk_test_ for non-production preview env, or document this clearly. The success path of /admin/bookings/{id}/charge-card was therefore not exercised in this iteration — fabricated cus_/pm_ ids cannot succeed against live Stripe.
Optional: harmonize charge-damages payload schema to match charge-card (raw dict + field-level 400 errors) so the admin UI gets a consistent error contract across all 3 charge endpoints.
critical_code_review_comments: Consent gating at routes/admin.py:772 raises 400 BEFORE the Stripe API call — verified by test_checkout_rejected_without_consent. Safe: no Stripe sessions created from a missing-consent payload.
routes/admin.py:842 writes consent_accepted_at as an ISO timestamp on the quote_requests row at checkout-create time. Good audit trail.

Finalize endpoint (admin.py:878) correctly uses params={'expand[]': ['payment_intent.payment_method']} in the httpx GET — this is the canonical Stripe form-encoded expand syntax, will produce ?expand[]=payment_intent.payment_method.
Finalize extraction logic at admin.py:894-913 handles both expanded-object and string forms of payment_intent / payment_method gracefully — defensive, won't crash if Stripe ever returns a bare id.

routes/payments.py:979 generic charge-card endpoint reuses the well-tested _stripe_off_session_charge helper, so the actual Stripe call path is identical to /charge-wait-time and /charge-damages — low risk of regression.
routes/payments.py:1011-1016 amount bounds ($0.50 min, $10k max) enforced via explicit 400s — both verified by tests.
routes/payments.py:1043 uses $push to extra_charges array — append semantics preserve history across multiple charges on the same booking. Good.

Frontend QuoteOfferConfirm.jsx:368 — pay button disabled state correctly tied to (paying || !consentAccepted), and handlePay re-guards with consentAccepted check at line 87 in case state desyncs. Belt-and-suspenders.

Frontend BookingDetailsDialog.jsx:834 wraps the entire charge-card-on-file-block in {canChargeOffSession && (...)} — hidden by design when wait_time_consent is false OR stripe_payment_method_id is missing. Source-confirmed conditional gate is correct.

Frontend BookingDetailsDialog.jsx:898 charge button disabled when (chargingExtra || !extraChargeAmount || description.trim().length < 4) — matches the brief's 'amount > 0 and description >= 4 chars' rule.
Reason dropdown options at BookingDetailsDialog.jsx:875-880: balance / extra_hour / extra_stop / tolls / gratuity / other — exact match to brief.
Main checkout regression: routes/payments.py:186 still emits payment_intent_data[setup_future_usage]=off_session and routes/payments.py:181 still emits customer_creation=always — no regression.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on tested surfaces — /quote/{token} consent gate end-to-end (checkbox toggle + pay-button disable + POST body contains consent_accepted:true) and homepage wait-time-consent-block text/checkbox. Admin BookingDetailsDialog visually unverified (blank admin page with JWT-only auth) but source-confirmed.

test_credentials: Admin JWT minted directly with backend/.env JWT_SECRET (HS256, sub=support@turanelitelimo.com, role=admin, 1h exp) — bypasses 2FA email. Same approach as iteration 36.
seed_data_creation: test_iteration37_saved_cards_charge.py creates 1 quote_request per consent-gate test (occasion='TEST_iter37') and ~6 synthetic bookings (source='TEST_iter37') directly in Mongo to exercise charge-card validation. Session-scoped autouse cleanup fixture deletes all TEST_iter37 rows at the end. Two additional bookings (WITH_CARD bid=8c8014f4-... and NO_CARD bid=4c060960-...) were inserted manually during the Playwright session and NOT auto-cleaned — main agent can drop them with: db.bookings.delete_many({source:'TEST_iter37'}).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iteration37_saved_cards_charge.py -v — runs in ~2s, validates consent gate + charge-card validation + regression on charge-wait-time/damages. To exercise the success path of /admin/bookings/{id}/charge-card you need (a) sk_test_ Stripe key in backend/.env (currently sk_live_!) and (b) a real saved-card booking created end-to-end via Stripe test checkout. Cleanup: db.bookings.delete_many({source:'TEST_iter37'}) and db.quote_requests.delete_many({occasion:'TEST_iter37'}). Frontend testids used this iter: quote-consent-checkbox, quote-consent-label, quote-confirm-pay-button, quote-confirm-page, wait-time-consent-block, wait-consent-checkbox, charge-card-on-file-block, extra-charge-amount, extra-charge-reason, extra-charge-description, charge-card-on-file-btn.
rca of the issue: N/A — feature ships green on all tested paths. The single risk is the LIVE Stripe key in preview backend/.env: any real success-path test against /quote-offer/{token}/checkout will create a billable LIVE Stripe Checkout session. Mitigation: switch preview to sk_test_ before exercising the happy path, OR add a guard in the route that 400s if STRIPE_API_KEY does not start with sk_test_ when REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL points to the preview host.
